issues.title: Buttons must have discernible text
nextSteps: Add visible text, an aria-label, or an aria-labelledby reference that clearly names the button’s purpose.

issues.title: Elements must meet minimum color contrast ratio thresholds
issues.explanation: Ensure the contrast between foreground and background colors meets WCAG 2 AA minimum contrast ratio thresholds
nextSteps: Increase the contrast between text and background to meet at least a 4.5:1 ratio (3:1 for large text).

issues.title: <html> element must have a lang attribute
issues.explanation: Ensure every HTML document has a lang attribute
nextSteps: Add a lang attribute to the <html> element so assistive technology announces content in the right language.

issues.title: Images must have alternative text
issues.explanation: Ensure elements have alternative text or a role of none or presentation
nextSteps: Add meaningful alt text if the image communicates content.
Use empty alt text only if the image is decorative.
Avoid phrases like 'image of' unless needed for context.

issues.title: Form elements must have labels
issues.explanation: Ensure every form element has a label
nextSteps: Associate a visible <label> with the form field using the for/id attributes, or wrap the field in the label.

issues.title: Document should have one main landmark
issues.explanation: Ensure the document has a main landmark
nextSteps: Document should have one main landmark.

issues.title: All page content should be contained by landmarks
issues.explanation: Ensure all page content is contained by landmarks
nextSteps: All page content should be contained by landmarks.

issues.title: All page content should be contained by landmarks
issues.explanation: Ensure all page content is contained by landmarks
nextSteps: All page content should be contained by landmarks.

issues.title: All page content should be contained by landmarks
issues.explanation: Ensure all page content is contained by landmarks
nextSteps: All page content should be contained by landmarks.

issues.title: All page content should be contained by landmarks
issues.explanation: Ensure all page content is contained by landmarks
nextSteps: All page content should be contained by landmarks.

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review motion and animation comfort
humanReviewPrompts.question: Can users pause, reduce, or avoid motion that is not essential?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review flashing and rapid visual change risk
humanReviewPrompts.question: Does any content flash more than three times per second, or could it be adjusted so it doesn't?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review captions for audio/video content
humanReviewPrompts.question: Do all pre-recorded audio and video elements have accurate captions?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review transcripts for media content
humanReviewPrompts.question: Is a full text transcript available for audio-only or video-only content?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review keyboard-only usability
humanReviewPrompts.question: Can every interactive element and creative interaction be reached and operated using only a keyboard?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review cognitive load
humanReviewPrompts.question: Is the interface free of unnecessary complexity, jargon, or steps that could overwhelm someone under time pressure or with a cognitive disability?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review plain-language navigation
humanReviewPrompts.question: Are navigation labels, menu items, and instructions written in plain, unambiguous language?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review sound-based interaction alternatives
humanReviewPrompts.question: Is sound-based feedback or interaction also available through a visual or text alternative?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review touch target size
humanReviewPrompts.question: Are interactive touch targets at least 24 by 24 CSS pixels, or given adequate spacing if smaller?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review consistent help placement
humanReviewPrompts.question: If help mechanisms exist across multiple pages, do they appear in the same relative order each time?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review accessible authentication
humanReviewPrompts.question: Does authentication avoid relying solely on cognitive function tests such as memorizing passwords or solving puzzles, without an alternative?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review multilingual accessibility notes
humanReviewPrompts.question: If the project serves multiple languages, are accessibility labels, alt text, and instructions translated and reviewed by fluent speakers?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review bypass/skip navigation
humanReviewPrompts.question: Is there a way to skip repeated navigation blocks (a "skip to content" link, or landmark regions) so keyboard and screen reader users don't have to tab through the same menu on every page?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review focus order
humanReviewPrompts.question: Does tabbing through the page move focus in an order that matches the visual reading order and preserves meaning?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review focus visibility
humanReviewPrompts.question: Is there always a clearly visible focus indicator on the element that currently has keyboard focus, and is it never fully hidden behind other content?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review consistent navigation
humanReviewPrompts.question: If navigation menus repeat across multiple pages, do they appear in the same relative order each time?

humanReviewPrompts.title: Review multiple ways to find content
humanReviewPrompts.question: Is there more than one way to locate a page within the site (e.g. navigation menu plus search, or a sitemap), other than a single required sequence of steps?
